author: taratorio
content:
```
Erigon is alive and serving telemetry with 21 execution peers. Its execution
head is block 13,148 versus the network maximum of 38,999, a gap of 25,851
blocks. Erigon reports `eth_syncing=false` and 100%. Interpretation: it is
caught up to the stale fork-choice/head supplied by Prysm; those values do not
mean it has reached the live network head.

The immediate fault is in the **Prysm consensus initial-sync/P2P path**, not the
Erigon execution path. In the preceding 30 minutes, Prysm repeatedly reported
payload parent-hash mismatches, invalid parent roots, and periods with no usable
peers. Erigon emitted no error-level logs; its only warnings said that an
external CL had not called the Engine API recently.

The target is part of a wider restart/resync event. All Erigon execution
processes and the five external consensus clients paired with Erigon started at
approximately 10:20 UTC. Lodestar-Erigon and Nimbus-Erigon have already caught
up; Prysm-Erigon is the slowest remaining pair. The telemetry establishes the
coordinated restart but not what triggered it.

At the latest 30-minute rate, Prysm is gaining about 1,194 slots per 30 minutes
after accounting for the live chain's roughly 150-slot advance. That gives a
rough catch-up estimate of about 11 hours if the rate holds. This estimate is
low-confidence because peer quality and historical Gloas payload-envelope
processing can change the rate.
```

author: taratorio
content: Erigon pairs just got added by Stefan a few hours ago to gd7. Seems like Prysm is being a bit slow to do inital sync compared to other CL pairs for some reason. Might be worth checking out
